steps.title: Identify what the second sentence does
steps.content: The second sentence uses "however" to pivot from what astronomers are sure of (Betelgeuse will explode) to what they are not (when). It pins the uncertainty on Betelgeuse's largely unknown internal characteristics — framing a specific knowledge gap.

steps.title: Trace how the third sentence responds
steps.content: The third sentence introduces Morrow and colleagues, who tried to crack that gap using acoustic waves but concluded the method could not sufficiently reveal those internal characteristics. So the second sentence states the very problem their work tried (and failed) to solve.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• "It describes a serious limitation of the method used by Morrow and colleagues.": the second sentence says nothing about Morrow's method; the limitation is described later, in the third sentence. ✗
  • "It presents the central finding reported by Morrow and colleagues.": the second sentence is general background about astronomers' uncertainty, not a finding by Morrow. ✗
  • "It identifies the problem that Morrow and colleagues attempted to solve but did not.": the second sentence states the unknown-internals problem, and the third sentence shows Morrow tried and failed to resolve it. ✓
  • "It explains how the work of Morrow and colleagues was received by others in the field.": no reception or reaction is described anywhere in the text. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
